ogTitle: Thevenard vs Thompson Beach: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Thevenard and Thompson Beach.
heading: Thevenard vs Thompson Beach — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Thevenard, South Australia and Thompson Beach, South Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Thevenard has a lower overall climate risk score (34/100) compared to Thompson Beach (35/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Thevenard or Thompson Beach safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Thevenard has a flood risk score of 59/100, while Thompson Beach scores 35/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Thevenard scores 45/100 for bushfire risk. Thompson Beach scores 19/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Thevenard or Thompson Beach?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Thevenard has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.73% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
